data.narrative:
Terrestrial Energy is a publicly traded nuclear energy company founded by Simon Irish that has raised $280M in total capital. The nuclear energy sector comprises a small but growing comp universe of 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies, including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables—only 2 same-stage peers and 0 adjacent-stage or similar-tier companies were identified, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum threshold required for robust median estimation. Expanding the peer set to all 8 nuclear energy private companies would introduce substantial stage and business model heterogeneity, rendering an all-sector median unreliable.

Without a sufficient narrow peer set, triangulation via peer-median cannot support a valuation range. Alternative methodologies—including revenue/EBITDA multiples (if applicable margins exist), precedent transaction analysis within the nuclear subsector, or discounted cash flow analysis anchored to regulatory timelines and commercialization risk—would require additional data on comparable transaction multiples, target margins, or management financial projections. The small and specialized nature of the nuclear energy market limits traditional comparable company analysis and necessitates deal-specific or DCF-driven valuation approaches.
